observations.recommendation: The Inventory Committee and/or the personnel assigned in inventory taking diligently conduct the physical count of PPE items, provide a reliable RPCPPE in accordance with the prescribed format of NGAS Manual and submit the same to the Audit Team not later than January 31;
Municipal Treasurer diligently prepare and provide complete Property Cards in the prescribed format of NGAS Manual;
Municipal Accountant trace and reconcile the discrepancies of balances between the books of accounts against the RPCPPE, Ledger Cards and Lapsing Schedule; and
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer facilitate the periodic reconciliation of their respective reports/ records and make necessary adjustments, if any.

The Municipal Treasurer prepare a duly certified list showing the names of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the RPTAR or Taxpayer’s Index Card maintained at the MTO;
Furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the duly certified list to serve as a basis in recording the RPT and SET Receivable accounts in the books; and

The Municipal Accountant record the receivables on the amount due and collectible for the year based on the list furnished by the Municipal Treasurer in order to recognize in the books an accurate and verifiable data pursuant to the above Manual.
Inventory Committee submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and thereafter, regularly submit the report not later than July 31 and January 31 each year;
Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer (GSO)-Designate prepare the Stock Cards following the prescribed format in the Manual and submit the same to the Audit Team;
Municipal Accountant prepare the SLC following the prescribed format in the Manual and submit the same to the Audit Team; and
Accounting and Treasury offices reconcile their respective records of the inventory accounts.
The Municipal Accountant provide all necessary data/information to support the computation of depreciation expenses recognized in order to render a reliable detailed lapsing schedule.
Facilitate/expedite the general revision and updating of a CLUP in compliance with the above provisions.

The Municipal Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan (SB) expedite the updating and amendment of the Municipality’s LRC to increase the taxes and other fees pursuant to Sections 129 and 191 of RA No. 7160 to have additional revenue sources to fund the projects of the Municipality.

The Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials fast track and ensure the optimum utilization of the 20 per cent DF through the efficient implementation of all priority development projects and programs to attain social, economic and environmental development;

The BAC Chairperson ensure that the BAC Secretariat post all Invitations to Bid or Requests for Quotation, Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed with the approved contracts in the PhilGEPS, in conspicuous places in the Municipality’s premises and agency website, if available, within the prescribed period in compliance with the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and
Proofs of these posting such as the Certification from the BAC Secretariat and the printout copies from PhilGEPS should be included as supporting documents in the disbursement vouchers.
That Driver’s Trip Tickets are serially numbered and properly accomplished indicating all the necessary information therein for every trip undertaken; and
That every Trip are supported with Driver’s Trip Tickets.
Ensure that all Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) included in the LDRRM Plan are implemented and LDRRMF utilization are maximized to attain its goals for the benefit of the constituents.

The Management, through the Municipal Accountant, prepare the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format and LDRRMO to submit the same to the Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

The Municipal Treasurer:

Intensify tax collection campaign by sending follow-up letters/notices of tax collection to delinquent taxpayers to remind them of their tax obligations;
Avail of the remedies by administrative action through levy on real property or by judicial action as provided in RA No. 7160; and

The BAC Chairperson require the contractor to post another performance security for the above infrastructure projects to answer the faithful performance by the winning bidder of its obligations under the contract and is valid until the issuance by the Municipality of the Certificate of Final Acceptance pursuant to Section 39 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

All perfected contracts and purchase orders be submitted to COA within five days after perfection thereof for the immediate review of the reasonableness of the cost, the conduct of inspection and other necessary audit procedures.

The Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain the Stock Ledger Cards, Property Ledger Cards and Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stocks Ledger Cards so that a periodic reconciliation of the inventories and property records between these Offices will be facilitated.

Management submit a written justification for not initiating the claim on the property insurance from the GSIS to indemnify the damages on the vehicle’s repairs and maintenance expenses. Likewise, we recommended that Management investigate the incident to support the insurance claim or hold the person/s liable, if due to negligence, for the repair cost, as warranted.

The Management direct the KALAHI-CIDSS MIAC to submit the lacking documents for evaluation by the Audit Team. Likewise, submit a justification for the inclusion of some municipal officials in the MIAC even if they are not required as per Section 2.2.1.1 of the Revised Community-Based Infrastructure Manual Volume I.

That Management send demand letters to the ineligible beneficiaries for the refund of the amount received totaling ₱3,918,000.00 and ensure that these disbursed funds are collected and returned to DSWD.

That Management ensure that the five mechanisms and processes provided in JMC 2013-01 are in place in order to pursue gender mainstreaming.  It is also recommended that LGU use a portion of the GAD budget for payment of professional fees, honoraria and other services for gender experts or gender specialists engaged to strengthen GFPS capability by updating and enhancing their skills through continuing gender education customized according to their functions to fully equip the GFPS in the formulation of their annual GPBs within the context of their mandates and other GAD processes to ensure gender mainstreaming in their policies, programs and projects.

The Municipal Mayor submit a written explanation on the excessive usage of cell cards and to reduce the cell card allowance granted to all division heads and members of the Sanggunian to a reasonable amount.

That the Municipality through its Municipal Assessor undertake the necessary steps for the speedy titling of the parcels of land registered in its name in order that ownership of these lots can be validated and to protect the interest of the LGU from adverse claims of unscrupulous parties. Further, the land account should be updated to include the value of the lot amounting to ₱70,730.00 which had been inadvertently not taken up in the books.

That before granting any additional benefit based on DBM issuances, the Municipality, through its Municipal Budget Officer coordinate with Provincial Budget Officer with regards to the LGU’s appropriations and limitations in accordance with Section 325 (a) of RA No. 7160. Concerned Municipal officials should refrain from granting additional benefits to its employees if it does not conform to existing law, rules and regulations. All officials and employees concerned who received such benefit should refund the said amount immediately.

We recommended the Management through the concerned officials and the Municipal Accountant, who agreed to:
submit to the Audit Team proof or evidences of control over the recorded asset transfers from DPWH totaling ₱349,098,232.69, such as a copy of right-of-way documents and/or transfer certificates of title on the land components on which these assets were constructed, otherwise, prepare a JEV to reverse the entries made to record these assets, following the proposed adjusting journal entry:
prepare JEVs to record the transferred assets controlled by the component barangays to the barangay books pursuant to the Manual on Financial Management of Barangays;
confer with the DPWH to properly communicate the matter with the Department of Education with the end in view of properly recording the assets controlled by the elementary schools in the books of the agency;
prepare JEVs to recognize the unrecorded donated motor vehicle from a medical association, following the proposed adjusting journal entry:
prepare JEVs to adjust the erroneous recording on donated ambulance from DOH Central Visayas Regional Office, following the proposed adjusting journal entry:
compute and prepare JEVs to provide for depreciation expenses of the donated transportation equipment starting from the date of acquisition; and
submit to the Audit Team a copy of a Deed of Donation on the second-hand mini bus duly accepted by the Municipality; otherwise, prepare JEV to reverse the entry following the proposed adjusting journal entry:
coordinate with the donor of the second-hand mini bus to execute a valid written Deed of Donation. Ensure that the donated equipment is duly accepted by the donee, and that the fair value of the donated equipment be immediately determined as of the date of acquisition for proper recording thereof pursuant to IPSAS 23.

We recommended that Management instruct the:
Municipal Engineer, who agreed to ensure that all completed infrastructure projects, regardless of fund source, are issued with Certificates of Completion and immediately furnish the Municipal Accountant with copies of Certificates of Completion upon issuance thereof;
Municipal Accountant to prepare JEVs to recognize transfer the cost of CIP on the completed projects to their appropriate PPE account, following the proposed adjusting journal entry:
Municipal Accountant to prepare JEVs to recognize the subsidy income and expense on the completed infrastructure assets in the Trust Fund books, following the proposed adjusting journal entry:
Municipal Accountant, who agreed to ensure that all progress payments are debited to the Construction in Progress account; and
Municipal Accountant, who agreed to ensure that the cost of completed infrastructure projects from the TF are immediately transferred to the GF upon completion thereof, and that the Subsidy from National Government or Subsidy from Local Government Units accounts be credited amounting to the total project cost upon completion.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant, who agreed to:
prepare JEVs to correct the negative balances of the Due from/to Special Accounts, following the proposed adjusting journal entry:
prepare JEVs to correct the erroneous entries on the fund transfer of 20% NTA to the 20% Development Fund, following the proposed adjusting journal entry:
retrieve all debit and credit entries to the intra-agency accounts of all funds and prepare the necessary elimination journal entries;
prepare an elimination working trial balance and post the elimination journal entries to all affected accounts. Ensure that the intra-agency accounts are fully eliminated; and
revise and submit to the Audit Team the CY 2024 financial statements based on the restated amounts, including the elimination working trial balance, for further evaluation.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant, who agreed to:
review and analyze the accounts with negative balances, trace or identify the cause/s, and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the accurate balances of affected accounts in order to fairly present the financial statements; and
ensure that the general ledgers and trial balances of all books be reviewed on a monthly basis to detect and correct negative balances.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant, who agreed to:
submit to the Audit Team the revised CY 2024 Notes to the Financial Statements taken into consideration the required disclosure on the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account in the format as prescribed in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and the LDRRM Officer towards the preparation of subsidiary ledgers in accordance with Paragraph 5.1.12 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in aid of the preparation of the CY 2025 financial statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, who agreed to coordinate to:
initiate steps to speed up the updating of all RPTARs or Taxpayer’s Index Cards and the certified list of taxpayers as of January 1, 2025, either manually or in the ECPAC system, which may include, among others, creating a special project for this purpose, appropriating funds for its implementation, and hiring casual, contractual or temporary employees, or augmenting existing Plantilla personnel or Job Order (JO) workers with the right qualifications in accordance with the 2017 Revised Omnibus Rules on Appointments and Other Human Resource Actions (ORAOHRA) and/or Section 77 of RA No. 7160;
ensure that accruals on real property and special education taxes are made for CY 2025 and were based on the certified list of taxpayers and RPTARs or Taxpayer’s Index Cards as of January 1, 2025; and
submit to the Audit Team copies of the certified list of taxpayers as of January 1, 2025, once already available, for further evaluation thereof.

We recommended that Management:
instruct the Municipal Treasurer, who agreed to determine the detailed breakdown of the total undistributed RPT shares totaling ₱1,777,443.51 per barangay and immediately issue checks for the release thereof; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant, who agreed to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers on the Due to LGUs account pertaining to the component barangays’ share of RPT and maintain summary schedules for monitoring thereof.

We recommended and Management through the BAC agreed to:
submit a written explanation regarding the noted deficiencies, including a planned course of action on the deficiencies noted, for further evaluation;
require all winning bidders/contractors to indicate the conforme date in all NOA and NTP; and
ensure that the procurement timelines are faithfully observed in all procurement activities in accordance with Annex C of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
request the substitution of the retention money for each of the infrastructure projects with irrevocable standby letters of credit equivalent to the supposed retention money, provided, that the project was on schedule and was satisfactorily taken; otherwise, demand from each of the contractors for the collection of retention money equivalent to 10 per cent of the total contract cost;
require contractors of infrastructure projects totaling ₱79,934,508.50 who opted for the direct posting of retention money, the extension of validity period or, if already expired, the posting of another surety bond equivalent to the retention money, ensuring that the coverage of the bond be until the supposed date of final acceptance; and
submit a written justification on the noted deficiencies to the Audit Team, including all pertinent documents for the release of retention money as provided in Item 9.1.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for further evaluation.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management through the BAC Chairperson agreed to:
instruct the BAC Secretariat to fully comply with the posting of all Requests for Quotation, NOAs, and Contracts or Purchase Orders for goods procured through SVP and/or through other applicable alternative methods as prescribed in Section 54.2 and 54.3 if the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. Ensure that postings are made in the PhilGEPS website, in conspicuous places within the premises of the PE, and the agency website, if available, within the period as prescribed; and
ensure that proofs of posting, such as certifications from the BAC Secretariat and printout copies from the PhilGEPS website, are attached as supporting documents in the DVs.

We recommended that Management settle the resulting difference of ₱2,471,876.25 by implementing the provisions in Section 62 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and submit pictures on the repair on the deficiency noted.
Management submitted a written reply to the Audit Team on August 13, 2024, informing that the contractor favorably responded to make all the necessary repairs on the said project. The Municipal Engineer also committed to supervise the repair by the contractor to get satisfactory results.
One of the attachments in their written reply is a copy of a letter from the contractor to the Municipal Engineer dated August 12, 2024, the former committing to rectify the necessary corrections. Target completion of the said correction is 90 days.
No pictures of the repair were submitted by Management to the Audit Team, hence, the Audit Team maintains its audit recommendations and shall further evaluate on whether the repair has been done by the contractor.

We recommended and Management through the concerned office agreed to:
submit a reasonable justification to the Audit Team on the non- or delayed implementation/completion of each of these development projects;
ensure the implementation and completion of these projects during CY 2025 for the benefit of such assets to its constituents; and
ensure that infrastructure projects are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready before appropriating such in the annual budget pursuant to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
submit a written explanation on the above noted observations together with its proposed action plans or measures if warranted for evaluation by the Audit Team;
ensure that all JO workers are duly contracted with individual Job Order Contracts specifying therewith the terms and conditions which include, but not limited to: the name of the contract, an express declaration that there is no employer-employee relationship, the specific project or purpose of hiring, place of assignment, period of hiring, work schedule, scope of work, pay rates, and authority to deduct contributions, among others; and
establish and adapt written guidelines in the hiring of JO workers taking into consideration the necessity for each office, qualifications, duties/ responsibilities and limitations, pay structure, procedures on hiring, performance evaluation and retention, and absorption to Plantilla position, among others.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
issue an executive order for the formation of Fire Brigades on all municipal-owned buildings and structures that can accommodate at least 50 persons in compliance with Division 2 of the 2019 Revised IRR of RA No. 9514;
ensure that the members of Fire Brigades undergo trainings and practice drills on fire prevention, fire suppression, rescue, emergency medical services and related emergency response in compliance with Divisions 3 and 4 of the 2019 Revised IRR of RA No. 9514; and
coordinate with the Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP) to conduct a comprehensive inspection on all municipal-owned buildings, structures and facilities in order to identify non-compliance and potential fire hazards, implement appropriate measures to mitigate them effectively, and ensure compliance with issuance of appropriate documents, reports and certificates such as FSICs, among others.

We recommended and Management through the concerned official agreed to submit a written justification to the Audit Team on the above-noted deficiencies and cause the revision of EO No. 34, series of 2022 taken into consideration the noted deficiencies in the composition of the GFPS, TWG, Secretariat and non-creation of the GAD M&E Team, with the end in view of achieving full compliance with Section 4.1.A of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor through the:
GAD Focal Person agreed to confer with the PCW, the DILG, or other government authorities on the availability of capability trainings such as gender sensitivity training and HGDG, among others, as provided in Item 4.1(A)(3) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended and ensure that all elected officials and department heads to attend with the end in view of applying such capabilities towards the implementation of the Magna Carta of Women; and
Municipal Budget Officer agreed to appropriate sufficient funds for capability building programs on GAD.
